title: D² scope spec
description: Schema for contracts/auth-scopes/scopes.spec.json — the source-of-truth catalog that DcsvIo.D2.Auth.Scopes.SourceGen reads to emit the Scopes static class into DcsvIo.D2.Auth.Abstractions.

name.description: Dot-separated lowercase scope name. Each segment must start with a letter and contain only lowercase letters and digits. At least 2 segments. Anonymous scopes use the 'anon.*' namespace.

description.description: Free-form description rendered as XML doc on the emitted constant.

actionSensitivity.description: Drives audit verbosity, OTP step-up triggers, and impersonation defaults. See ActionSensitivity enum docs.

impersonationBlocked.description: When true, scope is stripped from impersonated tokens at Edge mint time (defense in depth). Meaningless on anonymous scopes — D2SCP005 warning fires.

additionalProperties.description: Role names or '*' wildcard. minItems: 1 — empty arrays are invalid config (use no entry instead).

grantedTo.description: Per-(OrgType, Role) grant matrix. Keys: OrgType name (Admin / Support / Customer / ThirdParty / Affiliate) or '*'. Values: array of Role names (Auditor / Agent / Officer / Owner) or ['*']. Required for non-anonymous scopes (D2SCP008); omitted on anonymous scopes (universal pre-auth grant by namespace convention).

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
